raw_response:
{
  "communicative_function": "self-reflective acknowledgment of a metacognitive distinction between automatic cognition and evidence-based assessment",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "automatic thoughts can diverge from what is supported by evidence",
    "evidence exists that can indicate what is true independently of subjective cognition",
    "it is possible and worthwhile to notice such divergences",
    "the speaker possesses the capacity for this noticing"
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "internally coherent; rests on standard distinctions in epistemology and cognitive psychology without contradiction",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": "the distinction must be applied to improve reasoning accuracy in design or interaction contexts, such as reducing unchecked anthropomorphic projections in AI systems",
  "conditions_for_misleading": "the statement is taken to imply automatic thoughts are invariably false or that evidence is always immediately accessible and unambiguous"
}
